title: Central Processing Unit (CPU) Architecture
summary: 9618 AS — CPU components, fetch-decode-execute cycle, registers, buses, and performance factors.
learningObjectives: Describe CPU components: ALU, CU, registers, cache.
Distinguish Von Neumann and Harvard architectures.

simpleExplanation.title: The brain of the computer
simpleExplanation.summary: The CPU executes instructions stored in memory. The FDE cycle fetches each instruction, decodes it, and executes the required operation.
steps: Fetch: PC → MAR → memory → MDR → IR; PC increments.
Decode: CU interprets opcode in IR.
Execute: ALU performs calculation or data transfer occurs.
Performance: clock speed, cores, cache size, word length, pipelining.

sections.content: **CPU architecture** (4.1) is core 9618 theory. Examiners require **register-level detail** in FDE descriptions — name **PC, MAR, MDR, IR, ACC** and state what each holds at each stage.

items: Von Neumann: single memory for data and instructions
Harvard: separate program and data memory (parallel fetch)
Cache: L1/L2/L3 reduce average memory access time
Pipelining overlaps FDE stages of successive instructions
